issue_title: Different behavior between Quarkus and Wildfly distribution: SQLException on POST returns HTTP 201 instead 500
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

if a component with a name longer than 255 is created through REST API, Keycloak 17 returns 201 Created instead 500 Internal Server Error.

This bug exists only if Keycloak is running with Quarkus. If Keycloak runs with Wildfly, the REST API correctly returns 500 instead 201.

The bahaivor between Quarkus and Wildfly distribution is inconstant. 

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak returns 500 Internal Server Error, because the component can't create.

issue_title: Quarkus-based distribution cannot use MariaDB with Galera replication due to XA transactions being unsupported
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Using the Quarkus-based version of Keycloak 17.0.0 with a MariaDB backend with Galera replication enabled fails to start. Galera does not yet support XA transactions which Keycloak uses.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start normally and create a database.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak fails to start with
```
WARN  [com.arjuna.ats.jta] (main) ARJUNA016061: TransactionImple.enlistResource - XAResource.start returned: ARJUNA016099: Unknown error code:0 for < formatId=131077, gtrid_length=35, bqual_length=36, tx_uid=0:ffffac120005:92f9:620a53be:0, node_name=quarkus, branch_uid=0:ffffac120005:92f9:620a53be:3, subordinatenodename=null, eis_name=0 >: javax.transaction.xa.XAException: (conn=140) This version of MariaDB doesn't yet support 'XA transactions with Galera replication'
```

### How to Reproduce?

- Set up a MariaDB with Galera replication enabled. No actual clustering is needed.
- Build Keycloak with `--db=mariadb`
- Start Keycloak using the Galera-replicated MariaDB as database

### Anything else?

I've tried disabling XA transactions using `quarkus.datasource.jdbc.transactions=enabled` in `conf/quarkus.properties` before building Keycloak, but still get the same error.
commit_message:
Change tx driver handling.

Introduce a non-tx driver for the vendors and map based on new build option transaction-tx-enabled

Closes #10191 and others.

Co-authored-by: Pedro Igor <pigor.craveiro@gmail.com>

issue_title: User search with LDAP federation not consistent
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When Keycloak is connected to an LDAP federation the user search is inconsitent, either in the GUI or via REST API.

See "How to reproduce".

### Version

15.0.02

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add an LDAP federation toward any LDAP containing a user named "Martin" with the following configuration:
   -  Edit mode : READ_ONLY
   - sync settings" all synchronizations disabled
   - cache settings : Cache Policy=DEFAULT
1. Open the administration console, go to "Users" sections
1. In the search field type "Ma" and click on the magnifier button
   ==> no result
1. In the search field type "Ma*" and click on the magnifier button
  ==> Martin is found and imported in Keycloak
1. click on the magnifier button again
  ==> no more result !!
1. In the search field type "Ma" and click on the magnifier button
  ==> Martin appears !


### Anything else?

I think the issue comes from:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/federation/ldap/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/ldap/LDAPStorageProvider.java#L361

The implementation does not match the API that tells :

>  If possible, implementations should treat the parameter values as partial match patterns(i.e. in RDMBS terms use LIKE). 

The implementation passes the search nearly as is.
To be consistent with local database search, LDAPStorageProvider should :
- Trim search expression of already present '*' chars
- Suffix search expression with '*'
- For performance reasons **not** prefix search expression  with '*', see  #10005

issue_title: The CodeQL analysis is broken due to the large content of the generated SARIF file
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The CodeQL analysis is broken due to the large content of the generated SARIF file.

### Version

17.0.0

### Anything else?

Ref: This is a follow up of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/10108#issuecomment-1036180254
commit_message:
The CodeQL analysis is broken due to the large content of the SARIF file (#10606)

The issue was originally caused by high number of flows paths per alert
generated by the LDAP federation module. That was identified taking the
SARIF file generated and running:

```
jq '.runs[0].results | map({query_id: .rule.id, numPaths: .codeFlows |
length})' java.sarif

```

Together we reduced the number of flows paths, adding optimizations to
skip some paths and avoid false alerts.

Co-authored-by: Bruno Oliveira da Silva <bruno@abstractj.com>

Closes #10203

Co-authored-by: Joshua Mulliken <joshua@mulliken.net>

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Successfully execute all Spring Boot tests on all adapter containers (jetty94, undertow, tomcat etc...)

### Actual behavior

Spring Boot Tests are failing on multiple adapter containers (jetty94, undertow, tomcat).

### How to Reproduce?

```mvn -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/other/springboot-tests/pom.xml clean test -Dadapter.container=jetty94 -Dtest=AccountLinkSpringBootTest```

### Anything else?

_No response_

Closes #10218

issue_title: RelayState max length not respected
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

According to 3.5.3 in the specification
https://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/v2.0/saml-bindings-2.0-os.pdf
"RelayState data MAY be included with a SAML protocol message transmitted with this binding. The valueMUST NOT exceed 80 bytes in length and SHOULD be integrity protected by the entity creating themessage independent of any other protections that may or may not exist during message transmission"
Keycloak does not respect this.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should respect the length restriction.

### Actual behavior

The relay state length restriction is not enforced

### How to Reproduce?

Test will be provided

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Locale Setting for Update Password Mail
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi there,

I currently have a problem with the language of the Update password Mail and would be happy, if somebody could help. :)

When a keycloak administrator chooses to send an Update Password mail to a user, who has not set any locale in the user profile, the mail seems to be send in the language derived from the Accept language header of the logged in administrator. 

In our context (the system will be administered by German speaking personnel, but will have international users) this would lead to the undesired effect that for example korean users will get German Update password mails.

According to https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-4583 the process to get the best locale for a user is
Locale cookie
User (if authenticated)
"Accept-Language" header
Realm default (if set)
English

I think this works perfectly for login, account, admin themes, but for email themes the order should maybe be 
User (if locale is set in user profile)
Realm default (if set)
English

Best Regards,
Kathrin

### Version

16.1.0

### Expected behavior

If the user attribute "locale" is empty, the user should receive the Update Password mail sent from the keycloak admin console in the realm default language (if existing) or in English.

### Actual behavior

If the user attribute "locale" is empty, the user seems to receive receive the Update Password mail sent from the keycloak admin console in the language that the admin sending the mail is sending in his/her Accept language header.

### How to Reproduce?

Prerequisites: 
- Internationalization is enabled for the realm. 
- Default language for the realm is set to e. g. English.
- Admin is logged into keycloak admin console
- Accept language header of the admin is e. g. German

1) Create user in keycloak without setting a value for the locale.
2) Send Update Password Mail via Credentials tab.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Realm localization translations are not available in the Account console
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Only translations from the theme properties are available in the Account console. Realm translations created in the Admin Panel (or through the Admin REST API) are not available in the Account console.

There is `msg` and `msgJSON` variables available in the account console theme `.ftl` file, but the content of the variables does not contain translations from the realm.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

Realm translations are available in the `msg` and `msgJSON` variables in the account console (`index.ftl` file) and they take precedence over translations from the account theme properties files.

### Actual behavior

Translations from the account them properties file is available in the `msg` and `msgJSON` variables in the account console (`index.ftl` file)

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add translation in the English locale in the Admin Panel -> Realm -> Localization, with the key `testAccountConsoleBug` and the value `Test value`
2. Add ${msg.testAccountConsoleBug} in the `account/index.ftl`
3. Apply theme changes (not required if theme caching is disabled)
4. Open account console page in the browser

**Expected result:** `Test value` is present on the page

**Actual result:** error is being shown because message with such a key does not exist

### Anything else?

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

I wrote a shell script is simply reproduce and display the error:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env sh

TOKEN=$(curl -s -d 'client_id=admin-cli' -d 'username=admin' -d 'password=admin123' -d 'grant_type=password' 'http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token' | jq -r '.access_token')
FLOW_ID=$(curl -s -XPOST -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" 'http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/partial-export?exportGroupsAndRoles=false&exportClients=false' |  jq -r '.authenticationFlows[] | select(.alias == "saml ecp") | .id')

curl -XPUT "http://localhost:8080/admin/realms/master/authentication/flows/${FLOW_ID}" \
  --ignore-content-length \
  --trace /dev/stderr \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":"'"${FLOW_ID}"'","alias":"saml ecp","description":"Ahx7vooheijoo7Zoh4aeXee1aem2aehoh9seigheK2yecong3ciegoot1cee1eidogh5kooxee7noh1iohoh3aid5Aif8iodoo2aLujeijaim6Xahrai3IerahngemaipeiSho5ii4ugh2aecaeve7aiSi6aiz5eeso5thafooyuN3ook7Aij7ieweequeiM9NeemaePhuquahiech9SawaeBu5akeechoo4faipho9ahtahcheeK1bu1ooFoo8r","providerId":"basic-flow","topLevel":true,"builtIn":true,"authenticationExecutions":[{"authenticatorConfig":null,"authenticator":"http-basic-authenticator","authenticatorFlow":false,"requirement":"CONDITIONAL","priority":10,"autheticatorFlow":false,"flowAlias":null,"userSetupAllowed":false}]}'
```

By default, curl would stop processing after 0 bytes of reading the body and mention that there is additional content.

```
* Excess found: excess = 586 url = /admin/realms/master/authentication/flows/d2df7f5d-f235-49a6-8402-c63ab1258003 (zero-length body)
```

Thats the reason, why I add `--ignore-content-length` to see the additional output. Then curl starts to just hangs like the Apache HttpClient in my test suite.

### Anything else?

If curl/Apache http client is waiting, I was thinking about that Keycloak is not going to terminate the server connection.

issue_title: The `/auth` base path should be adjusted in v17 docs pages
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In multiple places in the documentation can be found the base path as `/auth` but from v17 with the shift from WildFly to Quarkus this was changed to `/`. 

See the bp in the [Migration to quarkus guide](https://www.keycloak.org/migration/migrating-to-quarkus)
> - /auth removed from the default context path



### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior


On the [Admin Rest API docs page](https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/17.0/rest-api/index.html) for version 17, the base path should be `/` instead of `/auth`

**Visual proof:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8113120/155166125-dd4a3c12-5ad0-4785-9ae3-6e1fe5a69105.png)


On the [Server installation page](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_installation/index.html) in step 8, opening Keycloak via  a localhost URL as for version 17, the path should be adjusted:

**Visual proof:**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8113120/155166380-9ae21561-07a0-49b2-960a-649fb6f1c552.png)


### Actual behavior

When I'm running Keycloak in a container locally using the dev mode the Admin REST APIs returns with `404` when using the `/auth` base path from the docs.

Example response:
```json
// Get an client access token
{
    "error": "RESTEASY003210: Could not find resource for full path: http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token"
}
// List users from the master realm
{
    "error": "RESTEASY003210: Could not find resource for full path: http://localhost:8080/auth/admin/realms/master/users"
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Token contains old DB values with "Always Read Value From LDAP" mapper setting
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Using LDAP User Federation where all mappers have "Always Read Value From LDAP" setting for user-attribute-ldap-mapper, we are still getting old values from keycloak and not the values from the LDAP store.
This happens for edit Mode UNSYNCED and READ_ONLY. 
After Max Lifespan we get once the correct values from the LDAP server, then the next time again the tokens contain wrong old values from the keycloak database. 
Only if the CachePolicy is set to NO_CACHE, we get always the correct values from the LDAP.

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

After the cache lifetime is expired and keycloak gets new values from the LDAP store, than subsequent keycloak tokens should contain the new values from LDAP and not old values from the database.

### Actual behavior

After the cache lifetime is expired and keycloak delivers once the new values from the LDAP store and in subsequent responses again outdated values.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure UNSYNCED  user  federation with ldap and user-attribute-ldap-mapper with "Always read values from LDAP".
2. Set Cache Policy to  "MAX_LIFESPAN=60000"
2. Change user attribute in LDAP
3. Request new tokens from keyclaok
4. After 60000 ms the first token contains the updated values from the LDAP. The next response contains again the old value.


### Anything else?

_No response_

I wanted to install a Keycloak instance on a dev machine where the http-port 8080 is already in use.

The option --http-port is not supported when calling "kc start-dev".

Although the option is supported when calling "kc start", it doesn't really work because it not possible to disable strict https and I get redirected to the https url. This is a dev machine, so that I can't configure https there.

### Version

### Expected behavior

Being able to use --http-port when calling "kc start-dev" and being able to disable strict http when calling "kc start"

commit_message:
#10443 start-dev: parsing command lines parameters

kc.bat called "shift" twice after parsing "start-dev". But "start-dev" is only one parameter so that "shift" should be called only once or else further command line parameters won't be parsed correctly.

Closes #10443

issue_title: v17/Quarkus Charset issue with LinkedIn IdP
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm running LinkedIn IdP on Keycloak v17/Quarkus on a staging environment, and v16.1.1/WildFly on a production environment, with same configurations.

Everything is working fine on v16.1.1/WildFly, but on v17/Quarkus my first name which contains a `é` accent is not properly synched.

### Version

v17.0.0/v17.0.1

### Expected behavior

User data grabbed from LinkedIn response should be properly encoded.

### Actual behavior

First name is wrong

![linkedin-charset-issue](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1165758/155624532-f936b6f6-1d8e-4094-8ad1-887334ff7401.png)

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a linkedin account with a special character like `é` in the first name.
- Configure LinkedIn broker in Keycloak Quarkus v17.
- Login with LinkedIn in Keycloak.
- Check the created user in admin UI.
- First name has invalid characters instead of the special one.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

* [The README](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/tree/6801688dd475aaffb12b818da4c0c47cebce66d3#getting-started) refers to the docker.io registry: `docker run jboss/keycloak` (which does not seem to be built on Quarkus)

* The [Container documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/server/containers) refers to `quay.io/keycloak/keycloak-x`

I am happy to issue a PR fixing the README if that's of interest. On the other hand, I wouldn't know how to fix (that is: replacing the image name from `keycloak-x` to `keycloak`) in the documentation website.

issue_title: Password blacklists folder
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I'm trying to use the password-blacklists policy and the server is not recognizing my file.

Reading the documentation I see the default folder is: "${jboss.server.data.dir}/password-blacklists/". I know we don't have Jboss anymore and I did assume that the default folder would be "/opt/keycloak/data/password-blacklists/".

### Version

17

Should be able to find my blacklist file and let me add the policy.

Do not find my file and give me the error:

### How to Reproduce?

Try to add a password blacklist policy to a realm.

https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_password-policies

issue_title: Keycloak admin UI drops https port if --hostname is used
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

If I do
```
docker run --name keycloak -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin -p 8543:8443 -v "$(pwd)"/target/classes/server-keystore.jks:/etc/server-keystore.jks quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:17.0.0 start  --hostname=localhost --https-key-store-file=/etc/server-keystore.jks
```
and then go to `https://localhost:8543` to the Admin UI which is displayed correctly and then click on an option to administer Keycloak, it fails with `404` the link has something like `https://localhost/somepath`, without `:8543`.

Replacing `--hostname=localhost` with `--hostname-strict=false` fixes it.

Perhaps it is a misconfiguration issue

### Version

17.0.0

issue_title: Localization GET endpoints of sub realms causes HTTP 500 unknown error when using a token issued by master realm

`AdminPermissions.realms(session, auth.adminAuth()).isAdmin()`
the user’s session is searched at the sub realm, even if the user has a token issued by the master realm. So, the user session could not be found and a NPE is thrown.

### Version

15.0.2, 18-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Localization GET endpoints should also be accessible with a token issued by the master realm.

### Actual behavior

returns status 500 with error message "unknown error"

### How to Reproduce?

**Important:** Don't use the clients security-admin-console or admin-cli to reproduce the issue. Because the Admin API does not check the token content for these clients (it checks the actual user roles) - the bug does not occur in this case.

Get an access token for a user at the master realm with the role “manage-users”. Then call, with the provided access token, the following endpoints:

- GET base_uri/auth/admin/realms/master/users/: returns status 200 as expected
- GET base_uri/auth/admin/realms/master/localization/: returns status 200 as expected
- GET base_uri/auth/admin/realms/sub/users/: returns status 200 as expected
- GET base_uri/auth/admin/realms/sub/localization/: returns status 500 with error message "unknown error"


### Anything else?

Relates to KEYCLOAK-17387 ([PR 7940](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7940))

Proposed solution:
Change the role check to
`auth.requireAnyAdminRole();`
which does a similar role check, but searches the user’s session at the right realm. And also this is better readable imo 😊.

issue_title: Failure to upload the SARIF file on pull requests
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The recent changes in the CodeQL workflow included the usage of [CodeQL CLI](https://codeql.github.com/docs/codeql-cli/). The workflow was tested extensively in a separate organization called Keycloak PoC, and proved to work. We attempted to apply similar policies used in the Keycloak organization to the PoC environment, but unfortunately some settings were missing, leading to the error message below on pull-requests:

```
A fatal error occurred: Error uploading SARIF to '[https://api.github.com/repos/keycloak/keycloak/code-
scanning/sarifs](https://api.github.com/repos/keycloak/keycloak/code-scanning/sarifs)' from 
'/home/runner/work/keycloak/keycloak/processed-js-adapter.sarif'. REASON: HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden:::
{"message":"Resource not accessible by integration","documentation_url":"
[https://docs.github.com/rest/reference/code-scanning#upload-a-sarif-file]
(https://docs.github.com/rest/reference/code-scanning#upload-a-sarif-file)"}
```

Investigating more about, it was possible to replicate the issue in the PoC environment. The issue happens due to a permission issue. The `GITHUB_TOKEN` does not provide write access to security events for untrusted sources on pull-requests. In the current scenario, CodeQL CLI is considered the untrusted source, and that's the reason we get access is forbidden to upload the SARIF file.

We have a few alternatives:

1.  Create a personal access token with permission to write security events and add that to the GitHub action
2.  Create a GitHub app inside the Keycloak org and generate an access token with write access to security events.
3.  Explore the usage of the  `github/codeql-action` considering that it is considered a trusted source.

Alternatives 1 and 2, can work around the issue but using a personal access token, or an app access token would make our GH actions depend on this. Any change inside the org, or misconfiguration, and the service will stop to work again.

During the review of #10606 it was mentioned the undocumented feature ([https://github.com/github/codeql-action/issues/962](https://github.com/github/codeql-action/issues/962)) that  allows us to provide custom arguments to the `github/codeql-action`. After asking their maintainers for more guidance ([https://github.com/github/codeql-action/issues/977](https://github.com/github/codeql-action/issues/977)), it is doable to explore option 3 and convert the current setup to use the action.

### Expected behavior

- CodeQL checks passing on pull-requests and main branch
- Security issues reported for main and pull-requests

### Actual behavior

- CodeQL checks are passing when we merge the changes to main, but not on pull-requests.
- Security issues reported on main, but not for pull-requests

### How to Reproduce?

- Check the CodeQL failures on pull-requests
commit_message:
Failure to upload the SARIF file on pull requests (#10725)

The changes proposed here will resolve the issues we have with
pull-request after the merge of #10606. It creates 3 different
workflows to conditionally execute the job based on changes submitted.

A detailed explanation about the issue can be found on #10717.

Resolves #10717

issue_title: Attributes field can be stored with empty value in some cases
issue_body:
### Description

In some cases, like: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/component/ComponentModel.java#L39, it is possible to store entry with an empty value. It is possible to store empty value in three ways:

- null
- empty list
- list containing one null item

We should ideally avoid storing entries with an empty value, however, if this is necessary we should have all places unified in the way we store them.
